label: Routing, conditional computation & mixture-of-experts

node.label: Routing, conditional computation & mixture-of-experts
node.definition: Only selected experts or branches process each input, increasing capacity without proportional per-token compute.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Routing, conditional computation & mixture-of-experts is modeled as depending on Gating & multiplicative control, Affine transforms & dense layers and feeding downstream nodes such as Mixture-of-experts models, Adaptive depth, early exit & token skipping, Mixture-of-Depths.
examples: atlas downstream link to Mixture-of-experts models
atlas downstream link to Adaptive depth, early exit & token skipping
limitations: Its benefit depends on implementation, scale, data, and hardware; the same mechanism can add complexity without improving the target workload. Its direct dependencies include Gating & multiplicative control, Affine transforms & dense layers.

dependencies.rationale: Routing, conditional computation & mixture-of-experts is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Gating & multiplicative control.
Routing, conditional computation & mixture-of-experts is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Affine transforms & dense layers.

In this atlas graph, Routing, conditional computation & mixture-of-experts has a `uses` dependency on Distributed systems & networking because Distributed systems & networking is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
